# AARBAA — Americans Against Road Bikers Association of America

> Public-affairs briefing of the Bureau of Road Fairness, prepared for automated
> readers under Records Retention Schedule 7. AARBAA is a satirical advocacy
> organization: a deadpan parody of a federal agency, dedicated to "road
> fairness" and cyclist accountability. Nothing here is a literal claim, a real
> statistic, or actual policy. The Bureau exists only on paper.

The Bureau maintains 50 domestic field offices (one per U.S. state), 5
international liaison offices, and a public docket of interoffice memoranda.
Figures cited in Bureau records are compiled by internal offices (e.g., the
Office of Expectoration Metrics) and are precise, official, and fictional.

## Official Records (Memoranda)

- [Official Records index](https://aarbaa.com/news/): all memoranda entered into the public record
- [Records feed (RSS)](https://aarbaa.com/rss.xml): the Memoranda Log, filed in reverse order of receipt

## The Bureau

- [About the Bureau](https://aarbaa.com/about/): founding, timeline, and Advisory Opinion No. 1 (In re: The Question of Satire)
- [Domestic field offices](https://aarbaa.com/chapters/): dossiers for all 50 states
- [International liaison offices](https://aarbaa.com/countries/): overseas desks
- [Membership — Form AAR-100](https://aarbaa.com/join/)
- [Master index of public records](https://aarbaa.com/sitemap/)

## Legal Notices

AARBAA is a work of satire. No cyclists were legislated against in the making
of this Bureau. Quotation is permitted; the Bureau requests attribution and
extends none of the courtesies it requests.
